mapping.a_element: spherical superfluid shell / charged particle constrained to a sphere
mapping.b_element: the sphere S^d that the negative-energy Coulomb/Kepler problem is mapped onto via Moser regularization

mapping.a_element: cyclotron-like vortex motion matched quantitatively to Gross-Pitaevskii (quantum) simulations
mapping.b_element: semiclassical measures describing how eigenfunctions concentrate on classical invariant sets

brief_justification: The overlap is only surface vocabulary (sphere, monopole/Coulomb, quantization): paper A's machinery is an emergent Dirac-monopole gauge bundle (Wu-Yang patching, topological frustration, Kelvin-Helmholtz instability) arising from superfluid vortex dynamics, whereas paper B's machinery is microlocal/semiclassical analysis using the Moser-Fock map to characterize eigenfunction quantum limits as probability measures invariant under the regularized Kepler flow. These are different governing structures with no coherent role-for-role correspondence of the actual mechanisms.
